researched_category.notes: Neo4j is a native graph database platform for connected data. Product page describes it as 'the #1 Platform for Connected Data' and 'graph database'.

one_liner.value: Native graph database platform for storing, querying, and analyzing connected data using Cypher query language, available as self-managed software or fully-managed cloud service (AuraDB).
one_liner.notes: Pricing page shows AuraDB Free/Professional/Business Critical/Virtual Dedicated Cloud tiers.

methods.notes: Multiple auth methods depending on the surface: OAuth2 (client credentials) for Aura Management API; Basic auth (username:password) for Query API/HTTP API/Bolt; Bearer token for SSO/OAuth setups; API key (x-api-key header) for GraphQL Library API.

access.notes: AuraDB Free tier is always free with no credit card required (source: pricing page says 'No credit card or other payment method required'). Aura API is available to all tiers including free (source: Aura API auth page says 'All tiers can create Aura API credentials'). The Aura API changelog confirms it is GA for self-serve customers.

summary.value: Neo4j exposes multiple well-documented public APIs: (1) Aura API (REST) for managing cloud instances (create, delete, pause, resume, snapshot instances, manage tenants) using OAuth2; (2) Query API (REST, v2) for executing Cypher queries via HTTP, superseding the deprecated HTTP API; (3) HTTP API (REST, deprecated) for transactional Cypher queries; (4) GraphQL Library for auto-generated GraphQL APIs from type definitions; (5) Bolt binary protocol for official drivers. The Aura API has OpenAPI specs available for testing. Rate limits: 25 req/min for free, 125 req/min for paid tiers.

agent_interface.notes: Neo4j has an official MCP server (binary download, supports stdio and HTTP transport) documented at neo4j.com/docs/mcp/current/. Multiple Neo4j Labs MCP servers exist: Cypher, Memory, Aura Manager, Data Modeling. Aura Agent (preview) provides deployable agent endpoints via MCP and REST. Composio already has a Neo4j toolkit with 21 tools. Neo4j CLI (neo4j.sh) also provides agent skill installation.

rationale.value: Neo4j is fully buildable into an agent toolkit today. It has well-documented public REST APIs (Aura Management API, Query API), an official MCP server, and multiple SDKs/drivers. The free self-serve tier (AuraDB Free, no credit card required) lets any developer get started immediately. API credentials (OAuth2 client ID/secret) are available to all tiers without admin approval. Composio already hosts a Neo4j toolkit with 21 tools. No material blockers exist beyond standard API rate limits (25 req/min for free tier).

s1.quote: AuraDB Free $0 — Learn and Explore Graphs — No credit card or other payment method required
s1.title: Neo4j pricing

s2.quote: The Aura API uses OAuth 2.0 for API authentication. All tiers can create Aura API credentials.
s2.title: Authentication - Neo4j Aura

s3.quote: Unless authentication is disabled on the server, all requests must be authorized using the login credentials of a valid user. Request are authorized through an Authorization header. Both basic and bearer authentication are supported.
s3.title: Authorize requests - Query API

s4.quote: you must include an API key or JWT token. With an API key authentication provider: Add x-api-key: YOUR_API_KEY to the header of the request.
s4.title: Using your GraphQL API - Neo4j GraphQL Library

s5.quote: Neo4j MCP implements the Model Context Protocol (MCP), an open standard that lets applications connect to external data sources and tools. It bridges any MCP-compatible client and your Neo4j instance.
s5.title: Introduction - Neo4j MCP

s6.quote: The official MCP Server for Neo4j, is a local MCP server that is downloadable as a binary and works with all types of Neo4j Deployments - Neo4j Aura, Self Managed, Docker, Neo4j Desktop, and Neo4j Sandbox.
s6.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integrations for the Neo4j Graph Database

s7.quote: The Aura API allows you to programmatically perform actions on your Aura instances without the need to log in to the Console.
s7.title: Overview - Neo4j Aura

s8.quote: Aura API now available for Self Serve customers — General Availability (GA) of the Aura API for all customers with billing applied on the Aura console... API credentials section is available to start creating credentials.
s8.title: Aura API now available for Self Serve customers

s9.quote: The Query API allows to execute Cypher statements against a Neo4j server through HTTP requests. This API supersedes the deprecated HTTP API.
s9.title: Introduction - Query API

s10.quote: The Neo4j HTTP API allows to execute a series of Cypher statements against a Neo4j server through HTTP requests.
s10.title: Introduction - HTTP API

s11.quote: The Neo4j GraphQL Library is a highly flexible, low-code, open source JavaScript library that enables rapid API development for cross-platform and mobile applications.
s11.title: Introduction - Neo4j GraphQL Library

s12.quote: openapi: 3.1.0 info: title: Neo4j Query API description: The Neo4j Query API enables the execution of Cypher statements against a Neo4j server through HTTP requests.
s12.title: Neo4j Query API — Documentation, OpenAPI

s13.quote: When using HTTP transport mode, the Neo4j MCP server supports two authentication methods... Bearer token authentication... Basic authentication...
s13.title: HTTP Authentication - Neo4j MCP

s14.quote: AuraDB Free — $0 — Learn and Explore Graphs — Start Learning — No credit card or other payment method required
s14.title: Neo4j pricing

s15.quote: Securely connect your AI agents and chatbots (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, etc) with Neo4J MCP or direct API to run Cypher queries, inspect graph relationships, create nodes and edges, and analyze connected data through natural language.
s15.title: Neo4J MCP Integration for AI Agents | Composio

s16.quote: Once you are satisfied with your agent, you can... make it available externally via either an Aura API endpoint or an MCP server endpoint.
s16.title: Aura Agent - Neo4j Aura

limitations: Aura Agent is still in preview (EAP) and may have limited availability.
The older HTTP API is deprecated in favor of the Query API.
Free tier has rate limit of 25 requests per minute for the Aura Management API.
No direct evidence was found about Webhooks support being documented as a protocol — listed as 'webhooks' based on GraphQL Library subscriptions feature.
The docs for the 'Aura Agent' API endpoints were not specifically fetched beyond knowing they exist.
